heading: Dependent defined
label: 152 Dependent defined
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Revenue Procedures listed in a table under .section 1 of this title

Amendments2017—Subsec. (d)(5). amended par. (5) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of this subsection—Pub. L. 115–97“(A) payments to a spouse which are includible in the gross income of such spouse under section 71 or 682 shall not be treated as a payment by the payor spouse for the support of any dependent, and“(B) in the case of the remarriage of a parent, support of a child received from the parent’s spouse shall be treated as received from the parent.”2008—Subsec. (c)(1)(E). , added subpar. (E).Pub. L. 110–351, § 501(b)Subsec. (c)(3)(A). , inserted “is younger than the taxpayer claiming such individual as a qualifying child and” after “such individual” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 110–351, § 501(a)Subsec. (c)(4). , substituted “who can claim the same” for “claiming” in heading.Pub. L. 110–351, § 501(c)(2)(B)(ii)Subsec. (c)(4)(A). , substituted “Except as provided in subparagraphs (B) and (C), if (but for this paragraph) an individual may be claimed as a qualifying child by 2 or more taxpayers” for “Except as provided in subparagraph (B), if (but for this paragraph) an individual may be and is claimed as a qualifying child by 2 or more taxpayers” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 110–351, § 501(c)(2)(B)(i)Subsec. (c)(4)(C). , added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 110–351, § 501(c)(2)(A)2005—Subsec. (e). amended heading and text of subsec. (e) generally. Prior to amendment, text consisted of pars. (1) to (4) relating to special rule for divorced parents, requirements for divorced parents, definitions of custodial and noncustodial parent, and exception for multiple-support agreements.Pub. L. 109–1352004— reenacted section catchline without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, section consisted of subsecs. (a) to (e) relating to general definition of dependent, rules relating to general definition, multiple support agreements, special support test in case of students, and support test in case of child of divorced parents, etc., respectively.Pub. L. 108–3111986—Subsec. (a)(9). , substituted “section 7703” for “section 143”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1301(j)(8)Subsec. (d)(2). , substituted “section 151(c)(4)” for “section 151(e)(4)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 104(b)(3)Subsec. (e)(1)(A). , substituted “section 151(c)(3)” for “section 151(e)(3)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 104(b)(1)(B)1984—Subsec. (e). , amended subsec. (e) generally, and in substantially revising support test provisions, enacted par. (1) custodial parent exemption, former par. (1) declaring the general rule that where a child received over one-half of his calendar year support from parents who were divorced or legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate maintenance, or were separated under a written separation agreement and the child was in the custody of one or both parents for more than one-half of the calendar year, the child would be treated as receiving over half of his support from the parent having custody for a greater portion of the calendar year unless treated under special rule provision as having received over half of his support from the parent not having custody; enacted par. (2) release of custodial parent exemption for the year, former par. (2) declaring the special rule that parent without custody would be deemed as furnishing over half of the support where the decree of divorce or separate maintenance, or written agreement, covering the taxable year, provided that parent without custody should be entitled to the section 151 deduction for the child and such parent provided at least $600 calendar year support, or alternatively, such parent without custody provided $1,200 or more calendar year support and the parent with custody did not establish more support of the child than the parent without custody; redesignated as par. (3) former par. (4) provision respecting exception for multiple-support agreement, deleting former par. (3) respecting requirement of an itemized statement of expenditures to resolve more support claims; added par. (4) respecting exception for certain pre-1985 instruments; added par. (5) enunciating special rule for support received from new spouse of parent, deleting former par. (5) regulations prescription provision; and added par. (6) cross reference provision.Pub. L. 98–369, § 423(a)Subsec. (e)(6). , substituted “section 213(d)(5)” for “section 213(d)(4)”.Pub. L. 98–369, § 482(b)(2)1976—Subsec. (a)(9). , substituted “section 143” for “section 153”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1901(b)(7)(B)Subsec. (a)(10). , struck out par. (10) relating to descendents of a taxpayer, who were members of taxpayer’s household, before receiving institutional care.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1901(a)(24)(A)Subsec. (b)(3). , among other changes struck out “of the Canal Zone, or of the Republic of Panama” after “country contiguous to the United States,” and provisions relating to children born or adopted in Philippines.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1901(a)(24)(B)Subsec. (c)(4). , struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1906(b)(13)(A)Subsec. (d). , substituted “organization described in section 170(b)(1)(A)(ii)” for “institution (as defined in section 151(e)(4))”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1901(b)(8)(A)Subsec. (e)(2)(B)(i). , substituted “each” for “all”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 2139(a)Subsec. (e)(3), (5). , struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1906(b)(13)(A)1972—Subsec. (b)(3). substituted “citizen or national of the United States” for “citizen of the United States” in two places.Pub. L. 92–5801969—Subsec. (b)(2). inserted reference to foster children who satisfy requirements of subsec. (a)(9) of this section.Pub. L. 91–1721967—Subsec. (a). , inserted “or (e)” after “subsection (c)”.Pub. L. 90–78, § 1(b)Subsec. (e). , added subsec. (e).Pub. L. 90–78, § 1(a)1959—Subsec. (b)(2). provided that a child who is a member of an individual’s household if placed with such individual by an authorized placement agency for legal adoption by such individual shall be treated as a child by blood.Pub. L. 86–3761958—Subsec. (a)(9). , inserted “(other than an individual who at any time during the taxable year was the spouse, determined without regard to section 153, of the taxpayer)”.Pub. L. 85–866, § 4(a)Subsec. (b)(3). , among other changes, struck out provision that “dependent” does not include any individual who is not a United States citizen unless such individual is a resident of United States or of a contiguous country, or of Canal Zone or Panama, and inserted provision barring exclusion from definition of “dependent” any child of taxpayer, legally adopted by him, if, for taxable year of taxpayer, child’s principal place of abode is taxpayer’s home and child is member of taxpayer’s household, if taxpayer is United States citizen.Pub. L. 85–866, § 4(b)Subsec. (b)(5). , added par. (5).Pub. L. 85–866, § 4(c)1955—Subsec. (b)(3). Act , substituted “” for “”.Aug. 9, 1955January 1, 1956July 5, 1946

children.text: a qualifying child, or
a qualifying relative.

children.heading: Dependents ineligible
children.label: 1 Dependents ineligible
children.text: If an individual is a dependent of a taxpayer for any taxable year of such taxpayer beginning in a calendar year, such individual shall be treated as having no dependents for any taxable year of such individual beginning in such calendar year.

children.heading: Married dependents
children.label: 2 Married dependents
children.text: An individual shall not be treated as a dependent of a taxpayer under subsection (a) if such individual has made a joint return with the individual’s spouse under section 6013 for the taxable year beginning in the calendar year in which the taxable year of the taxpayer begins.

children.heading: Citizens or nationals of other countries
children.label: 3 Citizens or nationals of other countries

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “dependent” does not include an individual who is not a citizen or national of the United States unless such individual is a resident of the United States or a country contiguous to the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: for the taxable year of the taxpayer, the child has the same principal place of abode as the taxpayer and is a member of the taxpayer’s household, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the taxpayer is a citizen or national of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: who bears a relationship to the taxpayer described in paragraph (2),

children.label: B
children.text: who has the same principal place of abode as the taxpayer for more than one-half of such taxable year,

children.label: C
children.text: who meets the age requirements of paragraph (3),

children.label: D
children.text: who has not provided over one-half of such individual’s own support for the calendar year in which the taxable year of the taxpayer begins, and

children.label: E
children.text: who has not filed a joint return (other than only for a claim of refund) with the individual’s spouse under section 6013 for the taxable year beginning in the calendar year in which the taxable year of the taxpayer begins.

children.label: A
children.text: a child of the taxpayer or a descendant of such a child, or

children.label: B
children.text: a brother, sister, stepbrother, or stepsister of the taxpayer or a descendant of any such relative.

children.label: i
children.text: has not attained the age of 19 as of the close of the calendar year in which the taxable year of the taxpayer begins, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is a student who has not attained the age of 24 as of the close of such calendar year.

children.heading: Special rule for disabled
children.label: B Special rule for disabled
children.text: In the case of an individual who is permanently and totally disabled (as defined in section 22(e)(3)) at any time during such calendar year, the requirements of subparagraph (A) shall be treated as met with respect to such individual.

children.heading: Special rule relating to 2 or more who can claim the same qualifying child
children.label: 4 Special rule relating to 2 or more who can claim the same qualifying child
i
children.text: a parent of the individual, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if clause (i) does not apply, the taxpayer with the highest adjusted gross income for such taxable year.

children.heading: More than 1 parent claiming qualifying child
children.label: B More than 1 parent claiming qualifying child
i
children.text: the parent with whom the child resided for the longest period of time during the taxable year, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the child resides with both parents for the same amount of time during such taxable year, the parent with the highest adjusted gross income.

children.heading: No parent claiming qualifying child
children.label: C No parent claiming qualifying child
children.text: If the parents of an individual may claim such individual as a qualifying child but no parent so claims the individual, such individual may be claimed as the qualifying child of another taxpayer but only if the adjusted gross income of such taxpayer is higher than the highest adjusted gross income of any parent of the individual.

children.label: A
children.text: who bears a relationship to the taxpayer described in paragraph (2),

children.label: B
children.text: whose gross income for the calendar year in which such taxable year begins is less than the exemption amount (as defined in section 151(d)),

children.label: C
children.text: with respect to whom the taxpayer provides over one-half of the individual’s support for the calendar year in which such taxable year begins, and

children.label: D
children.text: who is not a qualifying child of such taxpayer or of any other taxpayer for any taxable year beginning in the calendar year in which such taxable year begins.

children.label: A
children.text: A child or a descendant of a child.

children.label: B
children.text: A brother, sister, stepbrother, or stepsister.

children.label: C
children.text: The father or mother, or an ancestor of either.

children.label: D
children.text: A stepfather or stepmother.

children.label: E
children.text: A son or daughter of a brother or sister of the taxpayer.

children.label: F
children.text: A brother or sister of the father or mother of the taxpayer.

children.label: G
children.text: A son-in-law, daughter-in-law, father-in-law, mother-in-law, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law.

children.label: H
children.text: An individual (other than an individual who at any time during the taxable year was the spouse, determined without regard to section 7703, of the taxpayer) who, for the taxable year of the taxpayer, has the same principal place of abode as the taxpayer and is a member of the taxpayer’s household.

children.heading: Special rule relating to multiple support agreements
children.label: 3 Special rule relating to multiple support agreements
A
children.text: no one person contributed over one-half of such support,

children.label: B
children.text: over one-half of such support was received from 2 or more persons each of whom, but for the fact that any such person alone did not contribute over one-half of such support, would have been entitled to claim such individual as a dependent for a taxable year beginning in such calendar year,

children.label: C
children.text: the taxpayer contributed over 10 percent of such support, and

children.label: D
children.text: each person described in subparagraph (B) (other than the taxpayer) who contributed over 10 percent of such support files a written declaration (in such manner and form as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe) that such person will not claim such individual as a dependent for any taxable year beginning in such calendar year.

children.heading: Special rule relating to income of handicapped dependents
children.label: 4 Special rule relating to income of handicapped dependents
i
children.text: the availability of medical care at such workshop is the principal reason for the individual’s presence there, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the income arises solely from activities at such workshop which are incident to such medical care.

children.label: i
children.text: which provides special instruction or training designed to alleviate the disability of the individual, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which is operated by an organization described in section 501(c)(3) and exempt from tax under section 501(a), or by a State, a possession of the United States, any political subdivision of any of the foregoing, the United States, or the District of Columbia.

children.label: i
children.text: payments to a spouse of alimony or separate maintenance payments shall not be treated as a payment by the payor spouse for the support of any dependent, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of the remarriage of a parent, support of a child received from the parent’s spouse shall be treated as received from the parent.

children.heading: Alimony or separate maintenance payment
children.label: B Alimony or separate maintenance payment
i
children.text: such payment is received by (or on behalf of) a spouse under a divorce or separation instrument (as defined in section 121(d)(3)(C)),

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an individual legally separated from the individual’s spouse under a decree of divorce or of separate maintenance, the payee spouse and the payor spouse are not members of the same household at the time such payment is made, and

children.label: iii
children.text: there is no liability to make any such payment for any period after the death of the payee spouse and there is no liability to make any payment (in cash or property) as a substitute for such payments after the death of the payee spouse.

children.heading: Special rule for divorced parents, etc.
children.label: e Special rule for divorced parents, etc.
i
children.text: who are divorced or legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate maintenance,

children.label: ii
children.text: who are separated under a written separation agreement, or

children.label: iii
children.text: who live apart at all times during the last 6 months of the calendar year, and—

children.label: B
children.text: such child is in the custody of 1 or both of the child’s parents for more than one-half of the calendar year, such child shall be treated as being the qualifying child or qualifying relative of the noncustodial parent for a calendar year if the requirements described in paragraph (2) or (3) are met.

children.heading: Exception where custodial parent releases claim to exemption for the year
children.label: 2 Exception where custodial parent releases claim to exemption for the year
A
children.text: the custodial parent signs a written declaration (in such manner and form as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe) that such custodial parent will not claim such child as a dependent for any taxable year beginning in such calendar year, and

children.label: B
children.text: the noncustodial parent attaches such written declaration to the noncustodial parent’s return for the taxable year beginning during such calendar year.

children.label: i
children.text: a qualified pre-1985 instrument between the parents applicable to the taxable year beginning in such calendar year provides that the noncustodial parent shall be entitled to any deduction allowable under section 151 for such child, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the noncustodial parent provides at least $600 for the support of such child during such calendar year.

children.label: i
children.text: which is executed before ,January 1, 1985

children.label: ii
children.text: which on such date contains the provision described in subparagraph (A)(i), and

children.label: iii
children.text: which is not modified on or after such date in a modification which expressly provides that this paragraph shall not apply to such decree or agreement.

children.heading: Custodial parent
children.label: A Custodial parent
children.text: The term “custodial parent” means the parent having custody for the greater portion of the calendar year.

children.heading: Noncustodial parent
children.label: B Noncustodial parent
children.text: The term “noncustodial parent” means the parent who is not the custodial parent.

children.heading: Exception for multiple-support agreement
children.label: 5 Exception for multiple-support agreement
children.text: This subsection shall not apply in any case where over one-half of the support of the child is treated as having been received from a taxpayer under the provision of subsection (d)(3).

children.heading: Special rule for support received from new spouse of parent
children.label: 6 Special rule for support received from new spouse of parent
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, in the case of the remarriage of a parent, support of a child received from the parent’s spouse shall be treated as received from the parent.

children.label: i
children.text: a son, daughter, stepson, or stepdaughter of the taxpayer, or

children.label: ii
children.text: an eligible foster child of the taxpayer.

children.heading: Adopted child
children.label: B Adopted child
children.text: In determining whether any of the relationships specified in subparagraph (A)(i) or paragraph (4) exists, a legally adopted individual of the taxpayer, or an individual who is lawfully placed with the taxpayer for legal adoption by the taxpayer, shall be treated as a child of such individual by blood.

children.heading: Eligible foster child
children.label: C Eligible foster child
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(ii), the term “eligible foster child” means an individual who is placed with the taxpayer by an authorized placement agency or by judgment, decree, or other order of any court of competent jurisdiction.

children.label: A
children.text: is a full-time student at an educational organization described in section 170(b)(1)(A)(ii), or

children.label: B
children.text: is pursuing a full-time course of institutional on-farm training under the supervision of an accredited agent of an educational organization described in section 170(b)(1)(A)(ii) or of a State or political subdivision of a State.

children.heading: Determination of household status
children.label: 3 Determination of household status
children.text: An individual shall not be treated as a member of the taxpayer’s household if at any time during the taxable year of the taxpayer the relationship between such individual and the taxpayer is in violation of local law.

children.heading: Brother and sister
children.label: 4 Brother and sister
children.text: The terms “brother” and “sister” include a brother or sister by the half blood.

children.heading: Special support test in case of students
children.label: 5 Special support test in case of students
A
children.text: a child of the taxpayer, and

children.label: B
children.text: a student,

children.label: i
children.text: who is presumed by law enforcement authorities to have been kidnapped by someone who is not a member of the family of such child or the taxpayer, and

children.label: ii
children.text: who had, for the taxable year in which the kidnapping occurred, the same principal place of abode as the taxpayer for more than one-half of the portion of such year before the date of the kidnapping,

children.label: i
children.text: the deduction under section 151(c),

children.label: ii
children.text: the credit under section 24 (relating to child tax credit),

children.label: iii
children.text: whether an individual is a surviving spouse or a head of a household (as such terms are defined in section 2), and

children.label: iv
children.text: the earned income credit under section 32.

children.heading: Comparable treatment of certain qualifying relatives
children.label: C Comparable treatment of certain qualifying relatives
i
children.text: who is presumed by law enforcement authorities to have been kidnapped by someone who is not a member of the family of such child or the taxpayer, and

children.label: ii
children.text: who was (without regard to this paragraph) a qualifying relative of the taxpayer for the portion of the taxable year before the date of the kidnapping,

children.heading: Termination of treatment
children.label: D Termination of treatment
children.text: Subparagraphs (A) and (C) shall cease to apply as of the first taxable year of the taxpayer beginning after the calendar year in which there is a determination that the child is dead (or, if earlier, in which the child would have attained age 18).

children.heading: Cross references
children.label: 7 Cross references
children.text: For provision treating child as dependent of both parents for purposes of certain provisions, see sections 105(b), 132(h)(2)(B), and 213(d)(5).
